behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches expected interface (parameter names, types, return type)
behaviors.justification: Signature correctness depends entirely on what the real LLM generates. The LLM may produce syntactically invalid functions, use wrong parameter names, or misinterpret type requirements. A mocked LLM returning a pre-recorded good signature hides real failures where the actual LLM diverges. Only a real LLM call reveals whether the signature generation mechanism works across different requirements.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM with a natural language function description, extract and parse the generated function signature, verify that parameter names, types, and return type match the provided interface specification.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes all provided test cases on first or subsequent attempt
behaviors.justification: Code correctness depends on LLM's ability to understand requirements and generate sound logic. Syntax errors, logic errors, and test failures all trace back to LLM generation quality. Mocking the LLM and test execution would hide these real failures. Actual code execution (System prerequisite) combined with real LLM non-determinism (Agentic) is essential; the limiting factor is LLM quality, not code execution infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM to generate function code from the natural language description, execute the generated code against the provided test suite (actual Python execution), verify all tests pass or iteratively refine based on test failures.

behaviors.description: Refinement step includes failure messages in next LLM prompt for model to learn from mistakes
behaviors.justification: The core failure mode is the refinement mechanism itself: are failure messages being captured, formatted, and included in the next prompt? This can be tested at Integration level by mocking the LLM's first call to return intentionally failing code, capturing test failures, and inspecting the second LLM call's prompt arguments to verify failure context was included. Testing actual LLM improvement (Agentic) is valuable but not necessary to catch the primary failure (messages not included).
behaviors.test_description: Mock the LLM's first call to return code that fails the test suite, capture the test failure messages, trigger the refinement step, and verify that the second LLM call's prompt includes those failure messages as context.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error with all three attempts and their test results
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the control flow logic: does the retry loop stop at exactly 3 attempts? Does the error aggregation collect all results? Does the response structure match the spec? These are testable with mocked LLM and mocked test execution — the issue is not LLM quality or code execution, but whether the service's control flow and error handling work correctly. Mocks allow reliable, deterministic testing of this logic.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the LLM to return code that always fails tests, run the service, verify it makes exactly 3 generation/refinement attempts, then stops and returns a structured error response containing all 3 attempts with their test results.

behaviors.description: Full loop produces working implementation for function that single LLM call typically gets wrong
behaviors.justification: This behavior tests the value of iterative refinement: that multiple LLM calls with test feedback converge to a solution that a single LLM call cannot. This requires multiple sequential LLM calls where each refinement depends on previous test results. A single Agentic call (one LLM + test cycle) is insufficient; you need the complete iterative workflow with a real LLM to verify the loop actually produces working code for hard problems. Mocks would hide the real convergence behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a function description known to be difficult for single LLM calls, run the full service with real LLM and real test suite, verify that after iterative refinement (up to 3 attempts), the final generated code passes all provided tests.
